traps: The *Mult fields (OpsMult, dataOpsMult, networkActivityOpsMult, eventMult) are dropdowns whose option ids ARE the multiplier numbers ('1000000' = millions). They don't multiply unless paired with a count field (numberOfS3Ops, numberOfWriteEvents, etc.) — sending only multipliers produces $0 because there's nothing to multiply against.

CloudTrail's FIRST management trail is free. To get non-zero management-events cost set numberOfWriteTrails to >= 2. Data event trails (S3, Lambda, network activity) are paid from the first trail — modeling those is the cheapest way to a meaningful CloudTrail estimate.

There's both numberOfWriteEvents and numberOfWMEvents — they're different. WriteEvents/ReadEvents are CloudTrail management events (count of API calls); WMEvents is the input to Insights ('total management events to be analyzed for unusual activity'). Don't confuse them.

minimalConfig.description: S3 data events on one trail
